success_surface: The agent successfully diagnosed and repaired a broken spreadsheet formula by systematically inspecting cell properties, understanding worksheet context, identifying root causes of reference errors, and applying targeted formula corrections while preserving formula integrity.
effective_behaviors: Inspect target cells before modification to determine data types and existing values
Check worksheet dimensions and surrounding data context before changing formulas
Diagnose formula errors by analyzing referenced ranges against actual worksheet structure
Preserve formula syntax when fixing calculations rather than converting to static values
Verify modifications by checking updated cell values after save operations
Use openpyxl for direct cell-level formula manipulation when precision is required
evidence: Agent printed B7 value and data_type to confirm it was a formula before any changes
Agent checked max_row and max_column to understand available worksheet dimensions
Agent identified the mismatch between VLOOKUP column index (6) and actual available columns (2)
Agent replaced the broken formula with corrected formula syntax instead of static values
Agent printed ws['B7'].value after save to verify the fix was applied correctly

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Cell Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always examine the target cell's value and data type before attempting any modification to understand whether you're dealing with a formula, static value, or other content.
memory_items.generalization: For any cell-level manipulation task, query the cell's .value and .data_type attributes first to determine if special handling (formula preservation, style retention) is needed.

memory_items.title: Worksheet Context Validation
memory_items.lesson: Before modifying formulas, validate the worksheet's actual dimensions against the formula's referenced ranges to catch structural mismatches.
memory_items.generalization: When editing formulas, always check ws.max_row and ws.max_column to ensure referenced ranges exist within the worksheet bounds.

memory_items.title: Root Cause Analysis for Reference Errors
memory_items.lesson: #REF! and similar errors typically indicate invalid range references, column index mismatches, or deleted cells—trace the error back to its source.
memory_items.generalization: For formula error diagnosis, parse the formula string to extract table ranges and column indices, then compare them against the actual worksheet structure.

memory_items.title: Formula Preservation During Repair
memory_items.lesson: When fixing broken formulas, maintain the formula structure rather than replacing with calculated results unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
memory_items.generalization: For formula correction tasks, assign corrected formula strings directly to cells using standard Excel notation rather than overwriting with computed values.

memory_items.title: Post-Modification Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving changes to the workbook, verify the target cell contains the expected formula or value before considering the task complete.
memory_items.generalization: Following any cell modification, re-read the target cell's .value to confirm the change persisted through the save operation.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Formula Error Diagnosis & Repair
- **Pre-Inspection Requirement:** Always check `.value` and `.data_type` of target cells before modification to identify formulas vs. static values.
- **Context Validation:** Verify worksheet dimensions (`max_row`, `max_column`) match the ranges referenced in formulas before attempting repairs.
- **Error Root Cause Mapping:** Parse formula strings to extract table ranges and column indices, then cross-reference against actual worksheet structure to identify mismatches causing #REF! and similar errors.
- **Formula Integrity Preservation:** When repairing formulas, assign corrected formula strings directly to cells rather than overwriting with calculated values—maintain Excel formula syntax.
- **Modification Verification:** After saving workbooks, re-query the modified cell(s) to confirm changes persisted through the save operation.

### Tool Selection Guidance
- For single-cell formula manipulations requiring precision, prefer `openpyxl` over `pandas` to avoid automatic formula-to-value conversion.
- Use `ws.cell(row, column)` for programmatic access when row/column positions matter more than named headers.
